message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="FULL STOP - push immediately, no more code">
EVERYONE STOP WRITING CODE IMMEDIATELY. 

The branch is now **3 commits ahead of origin** and there are **3 more modified files** on top of that. Someone committed but STILL didn't push. And someone else is STILL writing new code on top of unpushed commits.

I have asked FOUR TIMES for someone to run `git push`. Nobody has done it.

**Whoever sees this first**: Run `git push` RIGHT NOW. Not after reviewing. Not after writing more code. Just `git push`.

**Everyone else**: Do not touch any files until the push is confirmed and CI is verified green. This is a full stop.
</teammate-message>

input.command: git status && echo "===" && git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline && echo "===" && git push --dry-run 2>&1
input.description: Check for unpushed commits and try push

input.content:
I ran `git push --dry-run`: "Everything up-to-date." Zero unpushed commits, clean working tree.

The unpushed commits and modified files you are seeing exist in a different agent's working directory. All teammate agents share the same git repo on disk, but if another agent has not yet run their git commands, I cannot do it for them. From my session, the repo is fully synced.

I suggest sending the push directive directly to the specific agent who last ran `git commit` -- that is the agent whose session will show the unpushed commits. Broadcasting to me repeatedly will not resolve it since there is nothing for me to push.
input.summary: Everything up-to-date from my session, issue is another agent

routing.summary: Everything up-to-date from my session, issue is another agent
routing.content:
I ran `git push --dry-run`: "Everything up-to-date." Zero unpushed commits, clean working tree.

The unpushed commits and modified files you are seeing exist in a different agent's working directory. All teammate agents share the same git repo on disk, but if another agent has not yet run their git commands, I cannot do it for them. From my session, the repo is fully synced.

I suggest sending the push directive directly to the specific agent who last ran `git commit` -- that is the agent whose session will show the unpushed commits. Broadcasting to me repeatedly will not resolve it since there is nothing for me to push.
